rationale: Despite a tier_1 IIIT Bangalore M.E. in Data Science, the actual career is Android mobile development at Hooli and cloud/DevOps infra at CRED with zero production ML or IR work, and skills listed as PEFT/LLMs 'advanced' for 54-59 months are inconsistent with that career history, indicating keyword inflation.

Career is entirely IT-services backend (.NET/full-stack at TCS and Wipro) plus a stint at fictional Stark Industries and frontend work at Swiggy — no production ML or IR experience, and AI exposure is self-learner level only.

Entirely non-technical career: Sales Executive at fictional Pied Piper, Project Manager at fictional Stark Industries, Customer Support at TCS, and Marketing Manager at Wipro — no engineering depth whatsoever, and 'advanced' Recommendation Systems skill listed for only 7 months is pure keyword inflation.

Directly relevant work at Salesforce: shipped XGBoost/LightGBM ranking models for a discovery feed and built a RAG pipeline with OpenAI embeddings stored in Pinecone, but experience is only ~3 years, education is tier_3 Symbiosis, and some skill durations (Haystack expert at 74 months) exceed total career length suggesting inflation.

Entire 8-year career is frontend and mobile development exclusively at IT-services firms (TCS, Cognizant, Mindtree) with no ML or IR work, and the Chemical Engineering background further distances this profile from the role's requirements.

Career is full-stack and DevOps at IT-services firms (Mindtree, Cognizant) plus QA automation at Zomato — no ML skills of substance, with top skills being PowerPoint (beginner) and SQL (beginner), and this candidate is flagged as a honeypot.

Exceptional fit: rebuilt candidate-JD matching pipeline achieving NDCG@10 from 0.72 to 0.91 serving 50M+ queries/month at Observe.AI, owned end-to-end ranking (BGE-large, Pinecone, LTR re-scoring) at Meta, and migrated BM25 to hybrid sparse+dense search at Apple, backed by IIT Hyderabad M.Sc and expert-level Python/BM25/Elasticsearch/NLP skills.

Relevant production experience at Amazon (ML pipelines, MLflow/Kubeflow) and Microsoft (XGBoost/LightGBM ranking models for discovery feed), plus summary describes building semantic search with sentence-transformers and FAISS; however, RAG listed as 'expert' for 79 months and Milvus for 76 months are impossible given a 4.9-year career, indicating significant skill inflation.

Entire career is DevOps at Cognizant and frontend at Capgemini — both explicit IT-services disqualifiers per the JD — with no production ML work despite excellent tier_1 education (IISc Bangalore Ph.D, IIIT Hyderabad M.E.).

Career spans two fictional companies (Wayne Enterprises, Stark Industries) and HCL IT services in DevOps/full-stack roles with no ML or IR work, Computer Vision as the primary ML skill is explicitly a negative signal for this NLP/IR-focused role, and education is tier_3.
